title: Learning to Better Search with Language Models via Guided Reinforced Self-Training

repos.name: snu-mllab__guided-rest
repos.evidence: README line 1-3: 'This is the code for the paper [Learning to Better Search with Language Models via Guided Reinforced Self-Training](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.02992) accepted to NeurIPS 2025.' Repo owner 'snu-mllab' matches Seoul National University ML Lab (authors Moon, Song). Contains recipe/countdown/ and recipe/code_repair/ implementing the Guided-ReST algorithm (core_algos.py, main_gen.py, main_data.py, main_eval.py), plus custom verl additions hgae/hppo (operation-level PPO from §3.3). Paper itself states 'We release the source code at https://github.com/snu-mllab/guided-rest' (paper_text.txt line 24).

rationale: The single cloned repo is the paper's official author release, confirmed by: (1) the README explicitly names this paper and its NeurIPS 2025 venue; (2) the GitHub org 'snu-mllab' corresponds to the Seoul National University ML Lab of the two SNU authors; (3) the paper itself cites https://github.com/snu-mllab/guided-rest as the code URL; (4) the repo contains the complete Guided-ReST pipeline (data generation, SFT, RL, evaluation) for both Countdown and code-repair tasks as described in the paper. The repo is a fork of the verl RL framework, but the paper's own contributions (recipe/ folders, hgae/hppo additions) are substantively present. All audit findings are correctly written against this repo—they concern missing baseline/ablation code and a training objective discrepancy, not a misidentification of the repo.
audit_impact: None. The audit correctly identified the core repo and wrote findings against it. No findings were written against a wrong repo. The paper's source-present classification is justified.
recommended_action: No action required. Audit is consistent and accurate.
